{'text': '群馬県高崎市石原町1247番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Obori-jinja}} [[JA:小祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11463293}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Obori Shrine |画像 = [[File:Obori-jinja haiden.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}} Ishihara-chō 1247 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|43.68|N|139|0|3.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Obori Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, copper plate roof |別名 = Obori Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 19|Q2523|WD=Q2523|1=April 19}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Obori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Obori-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Obori Shrine''' (おぼりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishihara-chō, {{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is “Mitsudomoe”{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Its old name in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} was “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following one[http://www.raijin.com/obori/html/yurai/htm Origin of Obori Shrine] (official site).. * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}},' the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} is {{ill|lt=Monju Bosatsu|Q471696|WD=Q471696|1=Manjushri}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. However, early-modern origin records mention {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}, and a Yakushi hall also existed behind the shrine{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. A document of {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2) describes the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} as stone{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Also, the 'Shikinaisha Survey Report' says there are 21 additional deities besides Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === History === === Founding === Founding is unknown. Nearby is Mishimazuka Kofun (a late 4th to early 5th century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}), and early Jōmon pottery fragments were excavated from the precincts, suggesting the shrine has stood here since ancient times{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. The clan that originally worshipped this shrine is unknown. The 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' records “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” in western Gunma district, and this “Sokusai-ji” is identified with {{ill|lt=Hikima Myōken-ji (Takasaki Hikima-chō)|Q11447022|WD=Q11447022|1=Myokenji Temple (Hikimacho, Takasaki City)}} near the Kōzuke provincial office. From this, the clan serving Obori deity is thought to have become provincial officials, and “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” is inferred to have been invited from this shrine as ujigami to Sokusai-ji built near the provincial office{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Overview history === Founding is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, there is a record that the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of “Obori-no-Kami” was promoted in {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Katōka District|Q11569784|WD=Q11569784|1=Kataoka District, Gunma}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} as “Obori Shrine,” and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. The same register gives the contemporary reading as “Ohafuri”{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō,' the Sōja text shows “Senior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin” as 7th among ten guardian shrines, while the Ichinomiya text shows it 4th, and the 'Gunsho Ruijū' text 6th, each as “Junior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin”{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. As related deity name, as noted above, “Junior Third Rank Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” appears in west Gunma district{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}}' compiled in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, “Jōzuke no Kuni kyūkasho daimyōjin” states “Nanamiya is Sawa-miya, called Koshi (likely mistaken for Obori), honji is Monju,” and the shrine is considered Kōzuke’s {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was granted Senior First Rank by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6){{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. In {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} (Kyōhō 2), the current main sanctuary was built{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. The {{ill|lt=early-modern|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} was Sekishō-ji{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}} (still adjacent today). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine-rank grants in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} era ** {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) May 25: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') * After Rikkokushi ** Junior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Ichinomiya text and Gunsho Ruijū text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** Senior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Sōja text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} (Shōtoku 6), Senior First Rank ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}}) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Precincts === [[File:Obori-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Main sanctuary (Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property)]] The current main shrine buildings consist of main sanctuary, heiden, and worship hall in an integrated form. However, the heiden and worship hall were built later than the main sanctuary. Documents and dedication plaques indicate reconstruction was planned in Shōtoku 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) and ridge-raising completed in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). The style is three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, with three bays on the sides and a three-bay front kōhai. The roof is now copper plate, though originally likely {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} or kokera-buki. Interior is divided into outer chamber (front two bays) and inner sanctuary (rear one bay), with a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} installed in the inner sanctuary. This main sanctuary is the oldest shrine main sanctuary in Takasaki, and its rear inset carved panel (oldest example in Gunma) serves as a chronological benchmark for similar works; with munafuda, dedication plaque, and donor inscription it is designated Takasaki City Important Cultural PropertyPrecinct explanation board.{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Cultural properties === === Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property === * Main sanctuary (attached: Kyōhō 1 munafuda, Kyōhō 2 dedication plaque, Kyōhō 4 donor inscription) (architecture) - designated Feb. 20, 2002[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2013121800679/ Obori Shrine Main Sanctuary, with attached Kyōhō-era records] (Takasaki City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Obori Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Katsuo Tajima|year=1984|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Hisayoshi Sakai|year=1986|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.13|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 10 Place Names of Gunma Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Basic Study of Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Obori-jinja}} * [http://www.obori-jinja.com/ Obori Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:高崎市の歴史]] [[Category:高崎市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{'text': '群馬県高崎市石原町1247番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Obori-jinja}} [[JA:小祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11463293}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Obori Shrine |画像 = [[File:Obori-jinja haiden.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}} Ishihara-chō 1247 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|43.68|N|139|0|3.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Obori Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, copper plate roof |別名 = Obori Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 19|Q2523|WD=Q2523|1=April 19}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Obori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Obori-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Obori Shrine''' (おぼりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishihara-chō, {{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is “Mitsudomoe”{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Its old name in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} was “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following one[http://www.raijin.com/obori/html/yurai/htm Origin of Obori Shrine] (official site).. * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}},' the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} is {{ill|lt=Monju Bosatsu|Q471696|WD=Q471696|1=Manjushri}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. However, early-modern origin records mention {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}, and a Yakushi hall also existed behind the shrine{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. A document of {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2) describes the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} as stone{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Also, the 'Shikinaisha Survey Report' says there are 21 additional deities besides Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === History === === Founding === Founding is unknown. Nearby is Mishimazuka Kofun (a late 4th to early 5th century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}), and early Jōmon pottery fragments were excavated from the precincts, suggesting the shrine has stood here since ancient times{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. The clan that originally worshipped this shrine is unknown. The 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' records “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” in western Gunma district, and this “Sokusai-ji” is identified with {{ill|lt=Hikima Myōken-ji (Takasaki Hikima-chō)|Q11447022|WD=Q11447022|1=Myokenji Temple (Hikimacho, Takasaki City)}} near the Kōzuke provincial office. From this, the clan serving Obori deity is thought to have become provincial officials, and “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” is inferred to have been invited from this shrine as ujigami to Sokusai-ji built near the provincial office{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Overview history === Founding is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, there is a record that the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of “Obori-no-Kami” was promoted in {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Katōka District|Q11569784|WD=Q11569784|1=Kataoka District, Gunma}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} as “Obori Shrine,” and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. The same register gives the contemporary reading as “Ohafuri”{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō,' the Sōja text shows “Senior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin” as 7th among ten guardian shrines, while the Ichinomiya text shows it 4th, and the 'Gunsho Ruijū' text 6th, each as “Junior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin”{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. As related deity name, as noted above, “Junior Third Rank Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” appears in west Gunma district{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}}' compiled in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, “Jōzuke no Kuni kyūkasho daimyōjin” states “Nanamiya is Sawa-miya, called Koshi (likely mistaken for Obori), honji is Monju,” and the shrine is considered Kōzuke’s {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was granted Senior First Rank by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6){{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. In {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} (Kyōhō 2), the current main sanctuary was built{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. The {{ill|lt=early-modern|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} was Sekishō-ji{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}} (still adjacent today). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine-rank grants in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} era ** {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) May 25: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') * After Rikkokushi ** Junior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Ichinomiya text and Gunsho Ruijū text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** Senior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Sōja text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} (Shōtoku 6), Senior First Rank ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}}) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Precincts === [[File:Obori-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Main sanctuary (Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property)]] The current main shrine buildings consist of main sanctuary, heiden, and worship hall in an integrated form. However, the heiden and worship hall were built later than the main sanctuary. Documents and dedication plaques indicate reconstruction was planned in Shōtoku 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) and ridge-raising completed in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). The style is three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, with three bays on the sides and a three-bay front kōhai. The roof is now copper plate, though originally likely {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} or kokera-buki. Interior is divided into outer chamber (front two bays) and inner sanctuary (rear one bay), with a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} installed in the inner sanctuary. This main sanctuary is the oldest shrine main sanctuary in Takasaki, and its rear inset carved panel (oldest example in Gunma) serves as a chronological benchmark for similar works; with munafuda, dedication plaque, and donor inscription it is designated Takasaki City Important Cultural PropertyPrecinct explanation board.{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Cultural properties === === Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property === * Main sanctuary (attached: Kyōhō 1 munafuda, Kyōhō 2 dedication plaque, Kyōhō 4 donor inscription) (architecture) - designated Feb. 20, 2002[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2013121800679/ Obori Shrine Main Sanctuary, with attached Kyōhō-era records] (Takasaki City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Obori Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Katsuo Tajima|year=1984|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Hisayoshi Sakai|year=1986|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.13|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 10 Place Names of Gunma Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Basic Study of Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Obori-jinja}} * [http://www.obori-jinja.com/ Obori Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:高崎市の歴史]] [[Category:高崎市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{'text': '群馬県高崎市石原町1247番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Obori-jinja}} [[JA:小祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11463293}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Obori Shrine |画像 = [[File:Obori-jinja haiden.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}} Ishihara-chō 1247 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|43.68|N|139|0|3.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Obori Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, copper plate roof |別名 = Obori Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 19|Q2523|WD=Q2523|1=April 19}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Obori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Obori-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Obori Shrine''' (おぼりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishihara-chō, {{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is “Mitsudomoe”{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Its old name in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} was “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following one[http://www.raijin.com/obori/html/yurai/htm Origin of Obori Shrine] (official site).. * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}},' the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} is {{ill|lt=Monju Bosatsu|Q471696|WD=Q471696|1=Manjushri}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. However, early-modern origin records mention {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}, and a Yakushi hall also existed behind the shrine{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. A document of {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2) describes the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} as stone{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Also, the 'Shikinaisha Survey Report' says there are 21 additional deities besides Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === History === === Founding === Founding is unknown. Nearby is Mishimazuka Kofun (a late 4th to early 5th century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}), and early Jōmon pottery fragments were excavated from the precincts, suggesting the shrine has stood here since ancient times{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. The clan that originally worshipped this shrine is unknown. The 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' records “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” in western Gunma district, and this “Sokusai-ji” is identified with {{ill|lt=Hikima Myōken-ji (Takasaki Hikima-chō)|Q11447022|WD=Q11447022|1=Myokenji Temple (Hikimacho, Takasaki City)}} near the Kōzuke provincial office. From this, the clan serving Obori deity is thought to have become provincial officials, and “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” is inferred to have been invited from this shrine as ujigami to Sokusai-ji built near the provincial office{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Overview history === Founding is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, there is a record that the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of “Obori-no-Kami” was promoted in {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Katōka District|Q11569784|WD=Q11569784|1=Kataoka District, Gunma}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} as “Obori Shrine,” and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. The same register gives the contemporary reading as “Ohafuri”{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō,' the Sōja text shows “Senior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin” as 7th among ten guardian shrines, while the Ichinomiya text shows it 4th, and the 'Gunsho Ruijū' text 6th, each as “Junior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin”{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. As related deity name, as noted above, “Junior Third Rank Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” appears in west Gunma district{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}}' compiled in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, “Jōzuke no Kuni kyūkasho daimyōjin” states “Nanamiya is Sawa-miya, called Koshi (likely mistaken for Obori), honji is Monju,” and the shrine is considered Kōzuke’s {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was granted Senior First Rank by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6){{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. In {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} (Kyōhō 2), the current main sanctuary was built{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. The {{ill|lt=early-modern|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} was Sekishō-ji{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}} (still adjacent today). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine-rank grants in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} era ** {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) May 25: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') * After Rikkokushi ** Junior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Ichinomiya text and Gunsho Ruijū text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** Senior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Sōja text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} (Shōtoku 6), Senior First Rank ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}}) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Precincts === [[File:Obori-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Main sanctuary (Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property)]] The current main shrine buildings consist of main sanctuary, heiden, and worship hall in an integrated form. However, the heiden and worship hall were built later than the main sanctuary. Documents and dedication plaques indicate reconstruction was planned in Shōtoku 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) and ridge-raising completed in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). The style is three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, with three bays on the sides and a three-bay front kōhai. The roof is now copper plate, though originally likely {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} or kokera-buki. Interior is divided into outer chamber (front two bays) and inner sanctuary (rear one bay), with a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} installed in the inner sanctuary. This main sanctuary is the oldest shrine main sanctuary in Takasaki, and its rear inset carved panel (oldest example in Gunma) serves as a chronological benchmark for similar works; with munafuda, dedication plaque, and donor inscription it is designated Takasaki City Important Cultural PropertyPrecinct explanation board.{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Cultural properties === === Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property === * Main sanctuary (attached: Kyōhō 1 munafuda, Kyōhō 2 dedication plaque, Kyōhō 4 donor inscription) (architecture) - designated Feb. 20, 2002[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2013121800679/ Obori Shrine Main Sanctuary, with attached Kyōhō-era records] (Takasaki City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Obori Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Katsuo Tajima|year=1984|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Hisayoshi Sakai|year=1986|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.13|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 10 Place Names of Gunma Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Basic Study of Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Obori-jinja}} * [http://www.obori-jinja.com/ Obori Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:高崎市の歴史]] [[Category:高崎市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{'text': '群馬県高崎市石原町1247番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Obori-jinja}} [[JA:小祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11463293}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Obori Shrine |画像 = [[File:Obori-jinja haiden.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}} Ishihara-chō 1247 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|43.68|N|139|0|3.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Obori Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, copper plate roof |別名 = Obori Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 19|Q2523|WD=Q2523|1=April 19}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Obori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Obori-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Obori Shrine''' (おぼりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishihara-chō, {{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is “Mitsudomoe”{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Its old name in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} was “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following one[http://www.raijin.com/obori/html/yurai/htm Origin of Obori Shrine] (official site).. * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}},' the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} is {{ill|lt=Monju Bosatsu|Q471696|WD=Q471696|1=Manjushri}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. However, early-modern origin records mention {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}, and a Yakushi hall also existed behind the shrine{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. A document of {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2) describes the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} as stone{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Also, the 'Shikinaisha Survey Report' says there are 21 additional deities besides Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === History === === Founding === Founding is unknown. Nearby is Mishimazuka Kofun (a late 4th to early 5th century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}), and early Jōmon pottery fragments were excavated from the precincts, suggesting the shrine has stood here since ancient times{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. The clan that originally worshipped this shrine is unknown. The 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' records “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” in western Gunma district, and this “Sokusai-ji” is identified with {{ill|lt=Hikima Myōken-ji (Takasaki Hikima-chō)|Q11447022|WD=Q11447022|1=Myokenji Temple (Hikimacho, Takasaki City)}} near the Kōzuke provincial office. From this, the clan serving Obori deity is thought to have become provincial officials, and “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” is inferred to have been invited from this shrine as ujigami to Sokusai-ji built near the provincial office{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Overview history === Founding is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, there is a record that the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of “Obori-no-Kami” was promoted in {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Katōka District|Q11569784|WD=Q11569784|1=Kataoka District, Gunma}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} as “Obori Shrine,” and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. The same register gives the contemporary reading as “Ohafuri”{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō,' the Sōja text shows “Senior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin” as 7th among ten guardian shrines, while the Ichinomiya text shows it 4th, and the 'Gunsho Ruijū' text 6th, each as “Junior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin”{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. As related deity name, as noted above, “Junior Third Rank Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” appears in west Gunma district{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}}' compiled in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, “Jōzuke no Kuni kyūkasho daimyōjin” states “Nanamiya is Sawa-miya, called Koshi (likely mistaken for Obori), honji is Monju,” and the shrine is considered Kōzuke’s {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was granted Senior First Rank by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6){{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. In {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} (Kyōhō 2), the current main sanctuary was built{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. The {{ill|lt=early-modern|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} was Sekishō-ji{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}} (still adjacent today). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine-rank grants in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} era ** {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) May 25: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') * After Rikkokushi ** Junior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Ichinomiya text and Gunsho Ruijū text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** Senior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Sōja text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} (Shōtoku 6), Senior First Rank ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}}) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Precincts === [[File:Obori-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Main sanctuary (Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property)]] The current main shrine buildings consist of main sanctuary, heiden, and worship hall in an integrated form. However, the heiden and worship hall were built later than the main sanctuary. Documents and dedication plaques indicate reconstruction was planned in Shōtoku 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) and ridge-raising completed in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). The style is three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, with three bays on the sides and a three-bay front kōhai. The roof is now copper plate, though originally likely {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} or kokera-buki. Interior is divided into outer chamber (front two bays) and inner sanctuary (rear one bay), with a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} installed in the inner sanctuary. This main sanctuary is the oldest shrine main sanctuary in Takasaki, and its rear inset carved panel (oldest example in Gunma) serves as a chronological benchmark for similar works; with munafuda, dedication plaque, and donor inscription it is designated Takasaki City Important Cultural PropertyPrecinct explanation board.{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Cultural properties === === Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property === * Main sanctuary (attached: Kyōhō 1 munafuda, Kyōhō 2 dedication plaque, Kyōhō 4 donor inscription) (architecture) - designated Feb. 20, 2002[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2013121800679/ Obori Shrine Main Sanctuary, with attached Kyōhō-era records] (Takasaki City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Obori Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Katsuo Tajima|year=1984|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Hisayoshi Sakai|year=1986|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.13|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 10 Place Names of Gunma Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Basic Study of Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Obori-jinja}} * [http://www.obori-jinja.com/ Obori Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:高崎市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Takasaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{'text': '群馬県高崎市石原町1247番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Obori-jinja}} [[JA:小祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11463293}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Obori Shrine |画像 = [[File:Obori-jinja haiden.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}} Ishihara-chō 1247 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|43.68|N|139|0|3.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Obori Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, copper plate roof |別名 = Obori Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 19|Q2523|WD=Q2523|1=April 19}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Obori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Obori-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Obori Shrine''' (おぼりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishihara-chō, {{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is “Mitsudomoe”{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Its old name in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} was “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following one[http://www.raijin.com/obori/html/yurai/htm Origin of Obori Shrine] (official site).. * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}},' the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} is {{ill|lt=Monju Bosatsu|Q471696|WD=Q471696|1=Manjushri}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. However, early-modern origin records mention {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}, and a Yakushi hall also existed behind the shrine{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. A document of {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2) describes the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} as stone{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Also, the 'Shikinaisha Survey Report' says there are 21 additional deities besides Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === History === === Founding === Founding is unknown. Nearby is Mishimazuka Kofun (a late 4th to early 5th century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}), and early Jōmon pottery fragments were excavated from the precincts, suggesting the shrine has stood here since ancient times{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. The clan that originally worshipped this shrine is unknown. The 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' records “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” in western Gunma district, and this “Sokusai-ji” is identified with {{ill|lt=Hikima Myōken-ji (Takasaki Hikima-chō)|Q11447022|WD=Q11447022|1=Myokenji Temple (Hikimacho, Takasaki City)}} near the Kōzuke provincial office. From this, the clan serving Obori deity is thought to have become provincial officials, and “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” is inferred to have been invited from this shrine as ujigami to Sokusai-ji built near the provincial office{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Overview history === Founding is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, there is a record that the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of “Obori-no-Kami” was promoted in {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Katōka District|Q11569784|WD=Q11569784|1=Kataoka District, Gunma}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} as “Obori Shrine,” and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. The same register gives the contemporary reading as “Ohafuri”{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō,' the Sōja text shows “Senior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin” as 7th among ten guardian shrines, while the Ichinomiya text shows it 4th, and the 'Gunsho Ruijū' text 6th, each as “Junior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin”{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. As related deity name, as noted above, “Junior Third Rank Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” appears in west Gunma district{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}}' compiled in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, “Jōzuke no Kuni kyūkasho daimyōjin” states “Nanamiya is Sawa-miya, called Koshi (likely mistaken for Obori), honji is Monju,” and the shrine is considered Kōzuke’s {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was granted Senior First Rank by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6){{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. In {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} (Kyōhō 2), the current main sanctuary was built{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. The {{ill|lt=early-modern|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} was Sekishō-ji{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}} (still adjacent today). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine-rank grants in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} era ** {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) May 25: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') * After Rikkokushi ** Junior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Ichinomiya text and Gunsho Ruijū text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** Senior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Sōja text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} (Shōtoku 6), Senior First Rank ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}}) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Precincts === [[File:Obori-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Main sanctuary (Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property)]] The current main shrine buildings consist of main sanctuary, heiden, and worship hall in an integrated form. However, the heiden and worship hall were built later than the main sanctuary. Documents and dedication plaques indicate reconstruction was planned in Shōtoku 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) and ridge-raising completed in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). The style is three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, with three bays on the sides and a three-bay front kōhai. The roof is now copper plate, though originally likely {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} or kokera-buki. Interior is divided into outer chamber (front two bays) and inner sanctuary (rear one bay), with a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} installed in the inner sanctuary. This main sanctuary is the oldest shrine main sanctuary in Takasaki, and its rear inset carved panel (oldest example in Gunma) serves as a chronological benchmark for similar works; with munafuda, dedication plaque, and donor inscription it is designated Takasaki City Important Cultural PropertyPrecinct explanation board.{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Cultural properties === === Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property === * Main sanctuary (attached: Kyōhō 1 munafuda, Kyōhō 2 dedication plaque, Kyōhō 4 donor inscription) (architecture) - designated Feb. 20, 2002[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2013121800679/ Obori Shrine Main Sanctuary, with attached Kyōhō-era records] (Takasaki City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Obori Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Katsuo Tajima|year=1984|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Hisayoshi Sakai|year=1986|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.13|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 10 Place Names of Gunma Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Basic Study of Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Obori-jinja}} * [http://www.obori-jinja.com/ Obori Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Takasaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{'text': '群馬県高崎市石原町1247番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Obori-jinja}} [[JA:小祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11463293}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Obori Shrine |画像 = [[File:Obori-jinja haiden.JPG|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}} Ishihara-chō 1247 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|43.68|N|139|0|3.60|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Obori Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, copper plate roof |別名 = Obori Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 19|Q2523|WD=Q2523|1=April 19}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Obori Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Obori-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Obori Shrine''' (おぼりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishihara-chō, {{ill|lt=Takasaki|Q336438|WD=Q336438|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is “Mitsudomoe”{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Its old name in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} was “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following one[http://www.raijin.com/obori/html/yurai/htm Origin of Obori Shrine] (official site).. * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}},' the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honji-butsu|1=UNKNOWN}} is {{ill|lt=Monju Bosatsu|Q471696|WD=Q471696|1=Manjushri}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. However, early-modern origin records mention {{ill|lt=Yakushi Nyorai|Q854773|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}, and a Yakushi hall also existed behind the shrine{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. A document of {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 2) describes the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}} as stone{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. Also, the 'Shikinaisha Survey Report' says there are 21 additional deities besides Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === History === === Founding === Founding is unknown. Nearby is Mishimazuka Kofun (a late 4th to early 5th century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}), and early Jōmon pottery fragments were excavated from the precincts, suggesting the shrine has stood here since ancient times{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. The clan that originally worshipped this shrine is unknown. The 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' records “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” in western Gunma district, and this “Sokusai-ji” is identified with {{ill|lt=Hikima Myōken-ji (Takasaki Hikima-chō)|Q11447022|WD=Q11447022|1=Myokenji Temple (Hikimacho, Takasaki City)}} near the Kōzuke provincial office. From this, the clan serving Obori deity is thought to have become provincial officials, and “Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” is inferred to have been invited from this shrine as ujigami to Sokusai-ji built near the provincial office{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Overview history === Founding is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official national histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, there is a record that the {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of “Obori-no-Kami” was promoted in {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' compiled in Enchō 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Katōka District|Q11569784|WD=Q11569784|1=Kataoka District, Gunma}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} as “Obori Shrine,” and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. The same register gives the contemporary reading as “Ohafuri”{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the 'Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō,' the Sōja text shows “Senior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin” as 7th among ten guardian shrines, while the Ichinomiya text shows it 4th, and the 'Gunsho Ruijū' text 6th, each as “Junior First Rank Obori Daimyōjin”{{Sfn|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}. As related deity name, as noted above, “Junior Third Rank Sokusai-ji Obori Myōjin” appears in west Gunma district{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Shintōshū|Q2616931|WD=Q2616931|1=Shintōshū}}' compiled in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, “Jōzuke no Kuni kyūkasho daimyōjin” states “Nanamiya is Sawa-miya, called Koshi (likely mistaken for Obori), honji is Monju,” and the shrine is considered Kōzuke’s {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Nanamiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was granted Senior First Rank by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 6){{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. In {{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}} (Kyōhō 2), the current main sanctuary was built{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. The {{ill|lt=early-modern|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} was Sekishō-ji{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=282}} (still adjacent today). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}, and in {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine-rank grants in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} era ** {{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618|1=880}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 4) May 25: from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper, rank-12 merit ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') * After Rikkokushi ** Junior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Ichinomiya text and Gunsho Ruijū text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** Senior First Rank ('Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Sōja text) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” ** {{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}} (Shōtoku 6), Senior First Rank ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}}) - written as “Obori Daimyōjin.” === Precincts === [[File:Obori-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Main sanctuary (Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property)]] The current main shrine buildings consist of main sanctuary, heiden, and worship hall in an integrated form. However, the heiden and worship hall were built later than the main sanctuary. Documents and dedication plaques indicate reconstruction was planned in Shōtoku 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) and ridge-raising completed in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). The style is three-bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, with three bays on the sides and a three-bay front kōhai. The roof is now copper plate, though originally likely {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} or kokera-buki. Interior is divided into outer chamber (front two bays) and inner sanctuary (rear one bay), with a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} installed in the inner sanctuary. This main sanctuary is the oldest shrine main sanctuary in Takasaki, and its rear inset carved panel (oldest example in Gunma) serves as a chronological benchmark for similar works; with munafuda, dedication plaque, and donor inscription it is designated Takasaki City Important Cultural PropertyPrecinct explanation board.{{Sfn|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}. === Cultural properties === === Takasaki City-designated Important Cultural Property === * Main sanctuary (attached: Kyōhō 1 munafuda, Kyōhō 2 dedication plaque, Kyōhō 4 donor inscription) (architecture) - designated Feb. 20, 2002[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2013121800679/ Obori Shrine Main Sanctuary, with attached Kyōhō-era records] (Takasaki City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Obori Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}|author=Katsuo Tajima|year=1984|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 11 Kantō|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=4560025118|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（神々）|1984年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Hisayoshi Sakai|year=1986|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.13|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（式内社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=Obori Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 10 Place Names of Gunma Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490107|ref={{Harvid|小祝神社（平）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Study Group|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Basic Study of Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Obori-jinja}} * [http://www.obori-jinja.com/ Obori Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obori Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Obori Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Takasaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]]
